workflow.name: Track documentation changes with Firecrawl, Postgres, Gemini, and Telegram
workflow.description:
This template watches an external documentation site every day and sends an AI-generated Vietnamese summary to Telegram whenever pages change. It is built around Postgres content hashing so it works with any Firecrawl deployment (Cloud or self-hosted) and any docs source, not just the default example.

## Who is this for?
Developers, API consumers, and integration maintainers who need to know the moment an upstream API or SDK documentation changes, so they can update their own code, community nodes, or client libraries without waiting for an announcement.

## How it works
- A Schedule Trigger runs daily at 9:00 AM
- Firecrawl **Map** enumerates every URL under the docs site, then a Code node dedupes and filters paths
- Firecrawl **Scrape** auto-loops one request per URL and returns clean markdown
- A Code node SHA-256 hashes the markdown of every page
- A single Postgres upsert query returns status `new`, `changed`, or `same` for each page
- Pages with real changes are aggregated and summarized by Google Gemini 2.0 Flash in Vietnamese
- The summary is delivered to Telegram; if nothing changed, the workflow ends silently

## Set up steps
- Setup takes around 15 to 20 minutes
- Self-hosted n8n required (the `n8n-nodes-firecrawl-v2` community node cannot be installed on n8n Cloud)
- Install the `n8n-nodes-firecrawl-v2` community node
- Create credentials for Firecrawl (Cloud or self-hosted), Google Gemini, Telegram Bot, and Postgres
- Run the `docs_snapshots` schema (SQL is in the main sticky note) on your Postgres database
- Edit the map URL and Telegram recipient to point at your docs source and chat
- On the first run, every page returns as `new` and you get one large alert listing all endpoints; subsequent runs only alert on real changes
- Detailed instructions are inside each sticky note in the workflow

parameters.content:
## Track documentation changes with Firecrawl, Postgres hashing, Gemini and Telegram

This workflow watches an external documentation site daily and sends an AI-generated Vietnamese summary to Telegram whenever pages change. It is built for Zalo Bot Platform docs (`https://bot.zapps.me/docs`) but works for any docs site by swapping the URL.

### How it works
1. A Schedule Trigger runs every morning at 9:00 AM Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh.
2. Firecrawl **Map** enumerates every URL under the docs site, then a Code node dedupes and keeps only docs paths.
3. Firecrawl **Scrape** fetches clean markdown for each page (auto-looped one request per URL).
4. A Code node SHA-256 hashes the markdown of every page.
5. A single Postgres query upserts hashes into `docs_snapshots` and returns a `status` of `new`, `changed`, or `same`.
6. A Filter keeps only `new` and `changed` pages, then an Aggregate combines them into one item.
7. If anything changed, Google Gemini 2.0 Flash reads the diff list plus previews and writes a Vietnamese summary of what changed and why it matters.
8. The summary is delivered to a Telegram chat. If nothing changed, the workflow ends silently.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Self-hosted n8n required (community nodes cannot be installed on n8n Cloud)
- [ ] Install community node `n8n-nodes-firecrawl-v2` via **Settings > Community Nodes**
- [ ] Create a Firecrawl credential (Cloud or self-hosted)
- [ ] Create a Google Gemini credential (free tier from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com))
- [ ] Create a Telegram Bot credential (token from @BotFather)
- [ ] Create a Postgres credential and run the schema below
- [ ] Edit the **Firecrawl Map Docs** and **Split & Filter Links** nodes to point at the docs site you want to monitor
- [ ] Set the Telegram recipient in the **Send Telegram Alert** node
- [ ] Activate the workflow

### Database schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE docs_snapshots (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  source VARCHAR(50),
  url TEXT,
  title TEXT,
  content_hash VARCHAR(64),
  content_preview TEXT,
  last_seen TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_changed TIMESTAMPTZ,
  UNIQUE(source, url)
);
```

### Why Postgres hashing instead of Firecrawl changeTracking
Firecrawl self-hosted does not expose `changeTracking` (Cloud only). Hashing markdown and upserting to Postgres works for any Firecrawl deployment and any docs source.

### First run
On the very first run every page returns status `new`, so you will receive one large alert listing every endpoint. Subsequent runs only alert on real changes.

### Customization
- Change the cron to hourly or weekly
- Watch multiple docs sites by duplicating the Map + Scrape branch per source
- Swap Gemini for OpenAI, Claude, or a local LLM
- Send alerts to Slack, Discord, Zalo, or email instead of Telegram

## Schedule and map docs URLs

A daily Schedule Trigger fires at 9:00 AM (Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh). Firecrawl **Map** enumerates every URL under the docs site, then a Code node keeps only docs paths and deduplicates them before the scrape loop.

## Scrape and hash each page

Firecrawl **Scrape** auto-loops one request per URL and returns clean markdown. A Code node SHA-256 hashes the markdown so the next step can detect any content change with a single comparison.

## Detect changes with Postgres

A single Postgres query upserts each page into `docs_snapshots` and returns a `status`:
- `new` if the URL is seen for the first time
- `changed` if the hash differs from the last stored value (`last_changed` is bumped)
- `same` if the hash is identical (only `last_seen` is refreshed)

The Filter keeps only `new` and `changed` rows, Aggregate gathers them into one item, and the IF branches on whether the list is empty.

## Summarize changes and alert

A Code node formats the changed pages into a markdown list with URL, status, and preview. Google Gemini 2.0 Flash reads the list and writes a concise Vietnamese summary covering what changed and which parts of downstream integrations may need updating. The final text is sent to Telegram.

## No changes path

If nothing changed, the workflow ends silently (no Telegram alert) so you only get pinged on real updates.

### Community node - Self-hosted only
This template uses the `n8n-nodes-firecrawl-v2` community node which requires self-hosted n8n. It cannot be installed on n8n Cloud. Both Firecrawl Cloud and self-hosted Firecrawl backends are supported.

parameters.query:
WITH old AS (
  SELECT content_hash AS old_hash FROM docs_snapshots WHERE source = $1 AND url = $2
),
upsert AS (
  INSERT INTO docs_snapshots (source, url, title, content_hash, content_preview, last_seen, last_changed)
  VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, NOW(), NOW())
  ON CONFLICT (source, url) DO UPDATE SET
    content_hash = EXCLUDED.content_hash,
    title = EXCLUDED.title,
    content_preview = EXCLUDED.content_preview,
    last_seen = NOW(),
    last_changed = CASE WHEN docs_snapshots.content_hash <> EXCLUDED.content_hash THEN NOW() ELSE docs_snapshots.last_changed END
  RETURNING url, title, content_hash AS new_hash, content_preview
)
SELECT
  upsert.url,
  upsert.title,
  upsert.new_hash,
  upsert.content_preview,
  COALESCE(old.old_hash, '') AS old_hash,
  CASE
    WHEN old.old_hash IS NULL THEN 'new'
    WHEN old.old_hash <> upsert.new_hash THEN 'changed'
    ELSE 'same'
  END AS status
FROM upsert
LEFT JOIN old ON true;

values.content:
=Bạn là kỹ sư bảo trì n8n community node `n8n-nodes-zalo-platform`. Tài liệu chính thức của Zalo Bot Platform (https://bot.zapps.me/docs) vừa có thay đổi. Hãy phân tích và viết tóm tắt tiếng Việt có dấu (tối đa 250 từ), gồm:

1. Liệt kê các thay đổi quan trọng nhất
2. Đoán xem thay đổi liên quan endpoint nào (sendMessage, sendPhoto, sendSticker, sendChatAction, getUpdates, setWebhook, deleteWebhook, getMe, getWebhookInfo)
3. Đánh giá ảnh hưởng tới node Zalo Bot hiện tại (cần update / cần thêm operation mới / không cần)
4. Đề xuất hành động cụ thể (file/method nào cần sửa)

Thống kê: {{ $json.totalChanged }} trang ({{ $json.newCount }} mới, {{ $json.changedCount }} thay đổi)

Danh sách:
{{ $json.changesList }}

Nội dung 5 trang đầu:
{{ $json.previews }}

user.name: THE NEXOVA
user.bio: We're a Strategic Operations & Implementation Partner - bridging Strategy to Execution for SMEs and foreign investors in Vietnam - from digital operations architecture to process automation. Creator of Firecrawl v2 node and CI automation tools.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Code node allows you to execute JavaScript in your workflow.

The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
